# Learnings

- Runtime services are defined under `src\DeskQuotes\Services\Implementations`; tests should import that namespace rather than the removed legacy `DeskQuotes.Services` duplicates to avoid ambiguous type references.
- Use the root `run-local.ps1` script for local workflows: `app`, `tests`, `unit-tests`, and `e2e-tests`. If no E2E project exists yet, the `e2e-tests` target prints a skip message and exits successfully.
- The wallpaper refresh hotkey and edit settings hotkey are both implemented via `GlobalHotkeyService`, which combines Win32 `RegisterHotKey` with a WinForms `IMessageFilter` because `TrayAppContext` is an `ApplicationContext` rather than a form with its own window handle. The service supports multiple hotkeys via a `Dictionary<int, Action>` keyed by hotkey ID; the message filter is registered once and shared across all hotkeys.
- `WallpaperRenderService` is registered as a singleton and now rotates through a curated dark background palette, so consecutive wallpaper refreshes use different dark colors without changing the output file path.
- `WallpaperBackgroundColorService` now owns wallpaper background state: normal refreshes still auto-pick from the dark palette, while tray/hotkey color actions pass an explicit background color for an immediate one-off refresh and then future normal refreshes continue auto-picking dark colors.
- Quote font selection now follows the same stateful pattern as quote/background handling: standard refreshes pick a random font from a curated Windows-safe list, while background-color-only refreshes reuse the current quote and current font so palette tweaks do not unexpectedly restyle the wallpaper.
- The `Change Wallpaper Font > Random Font (Ctrl + Alt + F)` tray/hotkey action is implemented via `WallpaperUpdateService.TryUpdateWallpaperWithRandomFont`, which preserves the current quote/background and always switches to a different curated font.
- `tests\DeskQuotes.UnitTests` now uses xUnit v3 packages and `Assert`/`Record` APIs for assertions; do not add `FluentAssertions` back into the unit test project.
- Quote configuration now supports an optional `Tags` property plus a top-level `tagCatalog` in `settings.json`; `Set Mood` uses those tags for quote eligibility, so each mood-filterable quote should tag itself with values drawn from `tagCatalog`.
- Selected mood state is persisted separately from `settings.json` under `%LocalAppData%\DeskQuotes\selected-mood.txt`; the tray app binds `tagCatalog` in configured order to build the `Set Mood` submenu, and wallpaper refreshes keep the current wallpaper with a warning when the selected mood has no matching tagged quotes.
- Tray context menu construction and styling now live in `src\DeskQuotes\Components\TrayContextMenuFactory.cs`; the menu keeps the native WinForms renderer, applies `Segoe UI` 10pt typography plus roomier padding, and groups `Edit Settings` between separators above and below it.
- The WiX MSI project uses a simplified per-user `%LocalAppData%\DeskQuotes` package: plain `<Files Include>` harvesting with suppressed per-user ICEs, `settings.json` authored as `Permanent="yes"` plus `NeverOverwrite="yes"` so upgrades preserve user edits, and shortcuts authored off the main executable file.
- The WiX project sets `OutputName=DeskQuotes`, so Release builds produce `src\DeskQuotes.MSI\bin\Release\DeskQuotes.msi`.
- The WiX `PublishDeskQuotes` target must run `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"`; otherwise MSI builds can silently reuse stale `obj\Release\publish` output and ship an older DeskQuotes executable than the current source tree.
- Startup now enforces a single running DeskQuotes instance via `SingleInstanceGate` and a named mutex from `AppConstants`; duplicate launches exit silently before the host, tray icon, timers, or hotkeys are created.
- App branding now lives in `src\DeskQuotes\Assets\AppIcon.ico`; the EXE embeds it via `ApplicationIcon`, the tray icon clones the embedded executable icon at runtime, and the WiX package reuses the same asset for shortcuts and Add/Remove Programs metadata.
- Successful global hotkeys now route through `HotkeyHudOverlayService`, which owns a small borderless `HotkeyHudOverlayForm`; the form uses `ShowWithoutActivation`, `WS_EX_NOACTIVATE`, rounded-region painting, and a timer-driven fade so keyboard-triggered actions show a bottom-center HUD without stealing focus or appearing in the taskbar. To avoid first-show lag, call `WarmUp()` during `TrayAppContext` startup so the hidden overlay handle is created before the first hotkey press, and keep extra horizontal slack in the text measurement/layout so medium-length labels like `Wallpaper refreshed` do not get ellipsized.
- Logging and telemetry are now configured from the existing `src\DeskQuotes\settings.json` file: use the top-level `applicationInsights.connectionString` value to enable Azure Application Insights, and use the `logging.logLevel` section for category log levels. Runtime diagnostics should flow through framework-injected `ILogger<T>` rather than ad-hoc logging APIs.
- Keep `Microsoft.ApplicationInsights.WorkerService` on the 2.23.x line unless the logging registration is rewritten too: `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ApplicationInsights` 2.23.x depends on `Microsoft.ApplicationInsights` 2.23.x, and mixing it with worker-service 3.1.x causes the `TelemetryContextExtensions` `TypeLoadException` at startup.
